summary: A 180-part queue for one FDM printer, spanning 6 filament materials over a queue horizon of 23,040 minutes (16 days around the clock; latest job deadline at minute 19,654). 1% of the parts would miss their due time even if started the moment they are released, so some late-delivery penalty is unavoidable. Outsourced partner-farm prints average 7.3x the in-house print cost.

reading.machine: one FDM 3D printer (capacity 1), starting from a clean/empty state
reading.setup: filament unload/purge/load between materials, plus a hardened-nozzle swap when switching to the abrasive PA-CF family (visible as the largest setup-matrix column)
reading.note: numbers are identical to the source CSV — only the semantics layer is new; all values are synthetic and reproducible from committed seeds, not production records
